title: Ratios and Rates
blurb: Placeholder — compare quantities using ratios, equivalent ratios, and rates including unit rate.

lessons.title: What Is a Ratio?
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3. Example: a bag holds 3 red marbles and 5 blue marbles.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — understand what a ratio is and how to write one in three forms.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. A ratio compares two quantities.

workedExamples.statement: Placeholder problem statement. Write the ratio of 4 apples to 6 oranges in simplest form.

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it problem. Write the ratio of 10 boys to 15 girls.

lessons.title: Equivalent Ratios
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — find equivalent ratios by multiplying or dividing both terms by the same number.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. Equivalent ratios describe the same relationship.

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it. Find two ratios equivalent to $3:5$.

lessons.title: Comparing Ratios
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — compare two ratios using a common denominator or equivalent fractions.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. To compare ratios, write them as fractions.

workedExamples.statement: Placeholder problem. Which is greater: $3:4$ or $5:7$?
workedExamples.solution: Placeholder solution. $3/4 = 0.75$ and $5/7 \approx 0.714$, so $3:4$ is greater.

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it. Which is greater: $2:3$ or $3:5$?
tryIt.solution: Placeholder solution. $2/3 \approx 0.667$ and $3/5 = 0.6$, so $2:3$ is greater.

lessons.title: Rates
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — define a rate as a ratio comparing two quantities with different units.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. A rate is a ratio with different units, like miles per hour.

workedExamples.statement: Placeholder problem. A car travels 120 miles in 3 hours. What is its rate?

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it. A runner runs 15 km in 2 hours. What is the rate?

lessons.title: Unit Rate
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — find and interpret the unit rate from a given rate.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. A unit rate has a denominator of 1.

workedExamples.statement: Placeholder problem. 5 cans of soup cost $\$6.25$. What is the unit rate?

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it. 8 notebooks cost $\$12$. What is the unit price?

lessons.title: Ratio Word Problems
lessons.concreteComparison: Placeholder — real-world opener authored in Step 3.
lessons.objective: Placeholder — solve word problems involving ratios and rates.
concept: Placeholder concept paragraph. Identify the two quantities being compared, then set up the ratio.

workedExamples.statement: Placeholder problem. The ratio of dogs to cats in a shelter is $3:2$. If there are 12 dogs, how many cats are there?

tryIt.statement: Placeholder try-it. The ratio of boys to girls is $4:5$. If there are 20 boys, how many girls?

practice.statement: Placeholder practice problem. Simplify the ratio $18:24$.

testYourself.statement: Placeholder test problem. A recipe calls for 2 cups of flour for every 3 cups of oats. How many cups of oats are needed for 8 cups of flour?
testYourself.solution: Placeholder solution. $8 \div 2 = 4$, so $4 \times 3 = 12$ cups of oats.
